archetype: Archetype 2: The Performance Pro (Tactical Minimalism / Industrial Control Room)
vibe: Rugged, precise, highly legible, high-tech industrial. Feels like software used in a multi-million dollar logistics facility.

layout_and_spacing.strategy: MODE B: THE CONTROL ROOM GRID
layout_and_spacing.container: min-h-screen bg-background text-text_primary p-4 md:p-6 lg:p-8 font-body

panels.sidebar: col-span-1 border border-zinc-800 bg-zinc-900/50 backdrop-blur-md p-6 flex flex-col gap-6 rounded-sm
panels.main_map: col-span-1 lg:col-span-3 border border-zinc-800 bg-black overflow-hidden relative min-h-[60vh] flex flex-col rounded-sm
panels.inventory_dashboard: col-span-1 lg:col-span-4 border border-zinc-800 bg-zinc-900/30 p-6 rounded-sm

layout_and_spacing.surface_styles: Flat solid surfaces. NO heavy glassmorphism for health/industrial tools to prevent refraction of data. Max 2px border radius (rounded-sm) or perfectly sharp (rounded-none).
layout_and_spacing.map_grid: The warehouse is a 30x30 grid. The CSS grid should be `grid-cols-[30]`. Use a wrapper with `overflow-auto` allowing the user to scroll through the massive facility, or use scale transforms for a zoom effect.

components.buttons: Sharp corners, no shadows. Primary: bg-[#FF3B30] text-white hover:bg-[#D32F2F] transition-colors. Secondary: bg-transparent border border-zinc-700 hover:bg-zinc-800.
components.inputs: Flat, bg-zinc-950 border border-zinc-800 focus:border-[#007AFF] focus:ring-1 focus:ring-[#007AFF] transition-all rounded-none font-mono text-sm.
components.tables: Dense, highly legible. Use JetBrains Mono for numbers. Hover states on rows must be a slight lightness bump (bg-zinc-800/50).
components.iot_nodes: Small rounded-full indicators. Pulse animation for active nodes.
components.map_cells: Empty cells: `border border-zinc-900 bg-zinc-950`. Stocked shelves: `border border-zinc-700 bg-zinc-800`. Target item: `bg-yellow-500`. Shortest Path: `bg-[#00E5FF] shadow-[0_0_10px_#00E5FF]`.

motion.hover: Instant or 150ms transitions (industrial interfaces should feel fast and immediate, no sluggish easing).
motion.path_animation: When a shortest path is found, stagger the appearance of the path blocks by 50ms using Framer Motion or simple timeout to simulate a radar 'tracing' the route.
motion.alerts: Use Sonner toasts that slide in sharply from bottom right. Sharp borders, black backgrounds.

pitch_hero.description: Wide-angle architectural rendering of a massive, dark industrial metal warehouse at night. Use as a low-opacity background behind a login/pitch entry screen with a gradient overlay.

metal_texture.category: Panel Backgrounds or Data Empty States
metal_texture.description: Macro photography of dark brushed steel plates stacked together. Good for decorative accents or sidebar headers.

raw_metal_texture.description: Shiny dark metal surface. Can be used for overlay blends (mix-blend-overlay) on structural elements.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. IMPLEMENT A DARK THEME EXCLUSIVELY: Add a massive CSS grid (`grid-cols-[30]`) for the 2D warehouse map. Ensure the map container has `overflow-auto` and a fixed height (e.g., `h-[60vh]`) so the 30x30 grid doesn't break the layout.

2. SYNTHETIC DATA GENERATOR: Create a 'Mock/Generate Data' button that fills the 900-cell grid with random metal sheet objects (about 500 sheets). An object should have: ID, Type (Aluminum, Steel, Copper), Size, Weight, Thickness, Material Grade, Location (x,y), and Stock Qty.

3. SEARCH & LOCATE: Provide a search panel. If duplicates are found, offer two UI states: 'Auto-select Nearest' (calculates distance from Worker Location to all targets and draws shortest path) or 'Show All' (highlights multiple cells). Default Worker Location is (0,0) or a draggable marker.

4. SHORTEST PATH LOGIC: Implement a basic Manhattan distance or A* pathfinding algorithm over the 30x30 grid to return an array of coordinate objects from Worker (0,0) to the Target (X,Y). Highlight these coordinates in the map grid in Cyan (#00E5FF).
5. DATA-TESTID: ALL buttons, search inputs, grid cells, and duplicate toggles MUST have `data-testid` attributes.
6. USE SHADCN UI: Incorporate shadcn/ui Select, Input, Button, Table, and Toast (sonner), but override radii to `rounded-sm` or `rounded-none`. Use `JetBrains Mono` for the data table.
